_meta.notes: alt1_vibe arrays are ordered primary-first (max 2). alt2_moment is used as both the timeline axis (Alt 2) and the 'when' filter (Alt 4). alt4_feeling is the second filter axis for Alt 4.

apps.title: Live "How Well Do You Know Us?" Trivia
apps.description: A live multiplayer quiz where every question is about the couple's real relationship, played on guests' phones with a leaderboard projected on the reception screen.
apps.why: Guests learn surprising things about the couple they didn't know, and the winner earns the right to give an actual toast — the game has stakes built in.

apps.title: Venue Scavenger Hunt with Their Love Story
apps.description: QR codes hidden around the venue each unlock a chapter of the couple's love story — cocktail hour becomes a treasure hunt through their actual lives.
apps.why: Every stop has a personal reason for being there, shy guests have something to do, and completing all chapters reveals a secret message from the couple.

apps.title: Anniversary Time Capsule
apps.description: Guests record short video messages sealed until the 1st, 5th, or 10th anniversary — the app locks them and delivers them automatically on the right date.
apps.why: A grandparent can leave a message for the couple to open when they might no longer be around; that possibility transforms how every guest records.

apps.title: Guest Bucket List Builder
apps.description: Guests add items to the couple's shared life bucket list live on their phones, growing on a display wall before becoming a formatted keepsake PDF.
apps.why: Instead of generic advice, guests are asked to imagine the couple's future — participatory, forward-looking, and the output is something the couple actually uses.

apps.title: Table-Specific Conversation Starter Cards
apps.description: Each table scans a unique QR code that serves conversation prompts tailored to that table's guest mix, shared history with the couple, and context notes.
apps.why: Guests feel like the couple thought of them specifically — the prompts reference their table's actual stories, not generic icebreakers.

apps.title: The Prediction Vault
apps.description: Guests submit sealed predictions about the couple's future grouped by year (1, 5, 10, 25 years out), unlocked on each anniversary when the couple scores them.
apps.why: It creates a recurring anniversary ritual — opening predictions with friends and scoring who was right becomes a party game that spans decades.

apps.title: Guest Memory Map
apps.description: Guests pin where they were in the world on the day of key couple milestones, adding a short note, building a live globe of the couple's people on the reception screen.
apps.why: Seeing pins appear in Tokyo, Berlin, and São Paulo in real time makes the global reach of someone's life genuinely visible — and unexpectedly moving.

apps.title: The Live Roast Board
apps.description: Guests submit anonymous roast jokes and funny memories; the MC approves them before they hit the display wall, where the crowd votes on the funniest one.
apps.why: It gives the funny friend a channel without the chaos of an open mic — structured, moderated, and the whole room laughs together without anyone having to stand up uninvited.

apps.title: The Couple's Unpopular Opinions Icebreaker
apps.description: The couple pre-loads their real unpopular opinions and guests vote agree/disagree in real time, revealing whether the room sides with the bride or groom on each take.
apps.why: Guests who don't know each other start debating 'pineapple on pizza' before they've finished their welcome drink — instant conversations, no awkwardness.

apps.title: The First Dance Reveal Ballot
apps.description: Guests vote on which of three first dances the couple will actually perform, with a live countdown clock ticking down before the winner is announced on screen.
apps.why: The whole room is invested in the outcome before the dance even starts — the anticipation does what choreography alone can't.

apps.title: Custom Wedding Bingo
apps.description: Every guest gets a unique bingo card with custom squares the couple wrote — 'best man trips on his speech,' 'DJ plays ABBA' — and taps them as they happen in real time.
apps.why: The squares are hilarious because the couple wrote them knowing exactly who will cry and what the DJ will play — and it makes every guest pay attention to everything, all night.

apps.title: The Guest Advice Oracle
apps.description: Guests respond to structured prompts — best marriage advice, a mistake to avoid, something you wish someone had told you — displayed as a live scrolling oracle wall.
apps.why: The output is genuinely useful (real wisdom from people who know you), and the couple can post a live question mid-reception and watch the room respond in real time.

apps.title: The Relationship Origin Story Exhibit
apps.description: A beautifully designed digital exhibit of the couple's story — photos, maps, milestones, voice notes — that guests explore on their phones like a mini-museum during cocktail hour.
apps.why: Guests who don't know the full story get genuinely drawn in — it's the 'how did you two meet?' story told properly, with the couple's own voice and photos.

apps.title: Design Our Future Home Map
apps.description: Guests pin places on an interactive map — the couple's meaningful spots, neighborhoods they should move to, places they must visit — filling live on the reception display.
apps.why: It becomes a living map the couple actually uses after: part artifact, part conversation piece, part shared life plan.

apps.title: The Collaborative Soundtrack
apps.description: Guests submit a song request paired with the memory it represents, votes shape the queue, and as each song plays its story appears on the display wall for everyone to read.
apps.why: The moment 'Dancing Queen' plays and a guest's lake-house memory appears on screen, everyone at that table loses it — the stories are what the music actually means.

apps.title: The Unprompted Love Letter Machine
apps.description: Guests submit secret messages or memories addressed to the bride, groom, or both; at a chosen moment the couple reads them live on screen for the first time, in front of everyone.
apps.why: The raw authenticity of a couple reading unscripted messages from people they love, in real time, is the moment guests most want to witness.

apps.title: Wedding Day Emotion Pulse
apps.description: Guests are prompted 5–6 times during the day to log how they're feeling, and the aggregate emotion is visualized live on the reception screen as a real-time pulse.
apps.why: Seeing the emotion spike during the vows and first dance — captured as a graph the couple keeps forever — is unexpectedly moving to look at years later.

apps.title: Table-to-Table Secret Relay
apps.description: Each table writes a secret message or dare for the next table, who must complete it and photograph the result before passing a new one on — a chain of challenges across the room.
apps.why: By the end of cocktail hour, tables that started as strangers have a shared photo and a story — the dare creates the connection no seating chart could engineer.

apps.title: The Personalized Cocktail Quiz
apps.description: Guests take a short personality quiz and are matched to a personalized cocktail from the wedding's custom menu, where every drink is named after a moment in the couple's story.
apps.why: It's a conversation starter at the bar, a way to share the couple's story through the drinks menu, and gives the bartender a live dashboard of what's trending.

apps.title: The Parallel Universe Game
apps.description: Guests submit what would have happened to the couple if they'd never met; the best entries are read aloud at dinner before the couple reveals their real 'almost didn't meet' story.
apps.why: It asks guests to think deeply about the couple's story, produces genuinely funny and touching entries, and the couple's real story is usually more dramatic than anyone guesses.
